Literature Digest

Apr 17 – May 01, 2026  ·  Chuong Lab, CU Boulder
840 scanned
13 preprints
20/20 journals
keyword + AI

AI Summary

This bi-weekly digest is dominated by mechanistic studies of nucleic acid sensing and isoform-specific innate immunity — themes that map directly onto the lab’s TE-driven IFN biology. Souaifan et al. demonstrate that loss of HP1 in mouse liver cells releases a coordinated burst of retrotransposon-derived RNAs and enhancer RNAs, and that low HP1 in hepatocellular carcinoma correlates with the same exosome-sensitive transcriptional program — a clean genomic illustration that retrotransposon RNA decay and enhancer activity are linked, with direct implications for the lab’s LTR10/cancer enhancer work. Bouton et al. dissect why the long ZAP isoform, but not the short one, fully decays CpG-rich viral RNA by recruiting KHNYN — a striking parallel to the lab’s IFNAR2-S decoy isoform, suggesting that long/short antiviral isoforms with opposing activities may be a recurring vertebrate motif worth surveying systematically.

A second cluster sharpens our picture of how host cells discriminate self vs non-self nucleic acids. Zhu et al. define a modular dsDNA recognition architecture in TREX1 that gates cGAS-STING activation; Zhang et al. show SFPQ shields endogenous dsRNA from MDA5 to preserve cell viability; and Gao et al. trace the cGAS-STING ancestor to a bacterial CBASS effector that shears membranes. Both TREX1 and SFPQ/MDA5 pathways are exactly where TE-derived nucleic acids would intersect with IFN signaling, making these strong context papers for any inflammasome or IFN narrative. Methodologically, CrossFilt offers a reciprocal-mapping filter for cross-species RNA-seq in primates that addresses alignment bias in the lab’s comparative transcriptomics, and the alternative splicing tools review is a useful current reference for ongoing exonization work.

Curated Highlights 20 papers

Top Journal Papers 20
Modular double-stranded DNA recognition defines specificity of the exonuclease TREX1 in cGAS-STING control
Apr 24, 2026 · Jiali Zhu, Lei Wang, Changjie Lin, Shuchen Bian, Xiaohan Luan, Yingying Sui, Luhang Piao, Wen Zhou · Immunity
TREX1 distinguishes itself from other DEDDh exonucleases via a modular dsDNA-recognition architecture (R128, sensing loop, auxiliary surface) that gates cGAS-STING activation. → article
The long isoform of ZAP coordinates multiple enzymes to mediate complete decay of target transcripts.
Apr 28, 2026 · Bouton Clément R, Swanson Chad M et al. · Cell Reports
The long ZAP isoform preferentially binds CpG-rich viral RNA and recruits KHNYN, EXOSC5, and decapping machinery to fully degrade target transcripts. → article
SFPQ prevents MDA5-mediated activation of innate immunity and preserves cell viability.
Apr 24, 2026 · Zhang Huirong, Damania Blossom et al. · Cell Reports
The splicing factor SFPQ shields endogenous dsRNA from MDA5, preventing aberrant type I IFN activation and preserving cell viability. → article
Divergent cis-regulatory haplotypes at Tlr2 are associated with immune responsiveness.
Apr 29, 2026 · Nandakumar Mridula, Råberg Lars et al. · Molecular Biology and Evolution
Divergent cis-regulatory haplotypes at Tlr2 in bank voles are under balancing selection and tune general immune responsiveness rather than ligand specificity. → article
A spatially coordinated keratinocyte-fibroblast circuit recruits MMP9⁺ myeloid cells to drive type I interferon-driven inflammation in photosensitive autoimmunity.
Apr 24, 2026 · Wang Yuqing, Garber Manuel et al. · Nature Immunology
MMP9+CD14+ myeloid cells produce IFN-beta in UVB-exposed skin and drive cutaneous lupus/dermatomyositis photosensitivity in coordination with keratinocytes and fibroblasts. → article
MYC-bound enhancer RNAs in cis regulate gene transcription and tumorigenesis.
Apr 24, 2026 · Li Sihan, Yang Da et al. · Science Advances
MYC binds cancer- and tissue-specific enhancer RNAs in cis to activate target genes and drive tumorigenesis. → article
Mapping transcription factor functions in astrocytes using in vivo gain-of-function Perturb-seq.
Apr 23, 2026 · Zhang Liansheng, Zhou Haibo et al. · Science
In vivo gain-of-function Perturb-seq in astrocytes builds a functional atlas of ~1000 transcription factors with cell-type-specific readouts. → article
Targeting of the nuclear RNA exosome to chromatin by HP1 affects the transcriptional programs of liver cells.
Apr 29, 2026 · Souaifan Hiba, Rachez Christophe et al. · Nature Communications
HP1 loss in mouse liver cells stabilizes retrotransposon RNAs and enhancer RNAs via reduced nuclear exosome targeting, mirroring HP1-low HCC tumors. → article
CrossFilt: a cross-species filtering tool that eliminates alignment bias in comparative genomics studies of primates
Apr 23, 2026 · Unknown · Genome Biology
CrossFilt retains only reciprocally-mapping reads across primate genomes to eliminate alignment bias in comparative RNA-seq quantification. → article
Tools and tactics for studying alternative splicing
Apr 17, 2026 · Rui Sousa-Luís, Maria Carmo-Fonseca · Nature Reviews Genetics
Review of current methods for detecting, quantifying, and functionally testing alternative splice isoforms across genetic variation and disease. → article
Gene flow from the European wild apple and selection shaped the domesticated apple genome.
Apr 20, 2026 · Chen Xilong, Cornille Amandine et al. · Current Biology
Whole-genome analysis of 218 apple genomes shows European wild apple gene flow plus selection on flowering-time loci shaped domestication. → article
Widespread gene-environment interactions shape the immune response to SARS-CoV-2 infection in hospitalized COVID-19 patients.
Apr 28, 2026 · Randolph Haley E, Barreiro Luis B et al. · Nature Communications
Single-cell eQTL mapping in 361K PBMCs from COVID-19 patients reveals widespread gene-environment interactions shaping cis-regulatory immune responses. → article
Evolutionary conservation and divergence of phagocytic and coagulation programs across bilaterian circulating immune cells.
Apr 27, 2026 · Li Yanan, Shao Changwei et al. · Molecular Biology and Evolution
Cross-phylum single-cell atlas of bilaterian circulating immune cells shows phagocytic and coagulation programs are deeply conserved with lineage-specific elaboration. → article
